---
category: productivity
title: File Organizer
name: file-organizer
description: "Use when organizing Downloads/Desktop folders, consolidating duplicates, creating naming conventions, or setting up archive strategies."
---

## Description

Create effective file organization with decision trees for archive strategy, naming conventions, and active/archive boundaries. Helps organize Downloads/Desktop and prevent chaos.

## When to Use

- Downloads or Desktop folder is chaotic
- Duplicate files need consolidation
- File structure no longer matches search patterns
- Setting up new project organization
- Archiving old projects
- Creating naming conventions

## When NOT to Use

- Version control systems (use git)
- Database organization
- Cloud storage (use cloud provider tools)
- Real-time file sync

## Workflow

### Phase 1: Audit Current State

- List all files and directories
- Identify duplicates
- Analyze search patterns
- Note pain points

### Phase 2: Design Structure

- Create decision tree for organization
- Define naming conventions
- Set active/archive boundaries
- Plan migration

### Phase 3: Implement Organization

- Create directory structure
- Move files to appropriate locations
- Rename files consistently
- Archive old items

### Phase 4: Maintain

- Document organization scheme
- Set up regular cleanup
- Monitor for chaos
- Adjust as needed

## Tools & References

- **Related Skills**: file operations, version control
- **Naming Conventions**: Consistent, searchable patterns
- **Archive Strategy**: When to keep vs delete

## Best Practices

- Organize by discovery pattern (date/project/type)
- Use consistent naming conventions
- Keep active and archive separate
- Document your system
- Regular cleanup and maintenance
- Avoid deep nesting (3-4 levels max)
- Use meaningful folder names
